tips.title: Be specific with context
tips.body: Instead of 'fix this bug', say 'fix the null pointer in auth.js line 42 — the user object is undefined when session expires'. More context = better results.

tips.title: Use role prompting
tips.body: Start with 'You are a senior TypeScript engineer reviewing this PR for security issues.' Role framing activates domain-specific knowledge patterns.

tips.title: Chain of thought
tips.body: Add 'Think step by step' or 'First analyze, then implement' to get more thorough reasoning before code generation.

tips.title: Constrain the output
tips.body: Specify format: 'Return only the function, no explanation' or 'Give me a bullet list of 5 options'. Constraints reduce noise.

tips.title: What is MCP?
tips.body: Model Context Protocol lets Claude use external tools — file systems, databases, APIs. Think of it as plugins for AI. Each server exposes tools Claude can call.

tips.title: Popular MCP servers
tips.body: filesystem (read/write files), github (PRs/issues), postgres (query DB), brave-search (web search), memory (persistent notes). Install via npx.

tips.title: Building custom MCP servers
tips.body: Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to build your own. Define tools with schemas, connect via stdio or SSE. Great for internal APIs and custom workflows.

tips.title: MCP security tips
tips.body: Always review tool permissions before approving. Use 'confirm writes' mode for MCP tools that modify data. Scope server access to specific directories.

tips.title: Plan before coding
tips.body: Use plan mode to have Claude analyze the codebase and propose an approach before writing code. Catches architectural issues early and saves iteration cycles.

tips.title: Iterative refinement
tips.body: Don't try to get everything in one prompt. Start broad, then refine: 'Now add error handling' → 'Add input validation' → 'Add tests'. Each step builds on the last.

tips.title: Use parallel mode for comparisons
tips.body: Open parallel mode to try different approaches side by side. Compare implementations, test strategies, or architectural patterns simultaneously.

tips.title: Session management
tips.body: Keep sessions focused on one task. Start a new session for unrelated work. Pin important sessions for quick access. Use search to find past conversations.

tips.title: Essential slash commands
tips.body: /clear resets context, /compact summarizes the conversation, /cost shows token usage. Use /help to see all available commands.

tips.title: Quick actions
tips.body: /review — get a code review. /fix — auto-fix issues. /test — generate tests. /explain — get an explanation. Most commands work with file context.

tips.title: Custom workflows
tips.body: Create reusable prompt templates in the toolbox. Combine with slash commands for one-click complex operations like 'refactor + test + review'.

tips.title: File attachments
tips.body: Attach files for context without pasting code. Claude sees the full file and can reference line numbers. Great for code reviews and debugging.

tips.title: What is CLAUDE.md?
tips.body: A markdown file at your project root that gives Claude persistent context — coding standards, architecture decisions, common patterns. Loaded automatically every session.

tips.title: What to include
tips.body: Tech stack, file structure, naming conventions, testing approach, deployment process. Include things you'd tell a new team member on day one.

tips.title: Keep it concise
tips.body: CLAUDE.md is included in every prompt — long files waste tokens. Aim for 200-500 lines. Use bullet points, not paragraphs. Link to docs instead of duplicating them.

tips.title: Example structure
tips.body:
# Project
## Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Prisma
## Conventions: kebab-case files, Zod validation
## Testing: Vitest + Testing Library
## Key dirs: src/app, src/lib, prisma/
